steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher claims some lakes have seen a decline in ice duration between the early 1980s and the mid-2000s. The answer must show a specific lake with fewer ice days in the mid-2000s than in the early 1980s.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Kitusjärvi is at a higher latitude than Lake Monona and typically had more days of ice per winter than Lake Monona did.: describes a latitude/typical-duration comparison, not a decline over time for one lake. ✗
  • Choice B — Lake Baikal had more days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it did in the winter of 1980–81.: shows an increase for Lake Baikal from 96 to 109 days, not a decline. ✗
  • Choice C — Kitusjärvi had fewer days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it did in the winter of 1980–81.: directly demonstrates a decline over the claimed period. ✓
  • Choice D — Kitusjärvi is at a higher latitude than Lake Monona and typically had fewer days of ice per winter than Lake Monona did.: describes a pattern unrelated to the early-1980s-to-mid-2000s decline. ✗
That matches Choice C — Kitusjärvi had fewer days of ice in the winter of 2005-06 than it did in the winter of 1980–81..
